From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

1990 film by Larry Cohen

For other uses, see [Ambulance (disambiguation)](/wiki/Ambulance_(disambiguation) "Ambulance (disambiguation)").

| The Ambulance | |
| --- | --- |
| Home media release poster | |
| Directed by | [Larry Cohen](/wiki/Larry_Cohen "Larry Cohen") |
| Written by | Larry Cohen |
| Produced by | Larry Cohen [Moctesuma Esparza](/wiki/Moctesuma_Esparza "Moctesuma Esparza") [Robert A. Katz](/wiki/Robert_A._Katz "Robert A. Katz") |
| Starring | [Eric Roberts](/wiki/Eric_Roberts "Eric Roberts") [James Earl Jones](/wiki/James_Earl_Jones "James Earl Jones") [Megan Gallagher](/wiki/Megan_Gallagher "Megan Gallagher") [Richard Bright](/wiki/Richard_Bright_(actor) "Richard Bright (actor)") |
| Cinematography | [Jacques Haitkin](/wiki/Jacques_Haitkin "Jacques Haitkin") |
| Edited by | Claudia Finkle Armond Leibowitz |
| Music by | [Jay Chattaway](/wiki/Jay_Chattaway "Jay Chattaway") |
| Production company | [Epic Productions](/wiki/Trans_World_Entertainment_(film_company) "Trans World Entertainment (film company)") |
| Distributed by | [Triumph Releasing Corporation](/wiki/Triumph_Releasing_Corporation "Triumph Releasing Corporation") |
| Release date | * October 19, 1990 (1990-10-19) |
| Running time | 95 minutes |
| Country | United States |
| Language | English |

***The Ambulance*** is a 1990 American [comedy thriller](/wiki/Comedy_thriller "Comedy thriller") film written and directed by [Larry Cohen](/wiki/Larry_Cohen "Larry Cohen"). It stars [Eric Roberts](/wiki/Eric_Roberts "Eric Roberts"), [Megan Gallagher](/wiki/Megan_Gallagher "Megan Gallagher"), [James Earl Jones](/wiki/James_Earl_Jones "James Earl Jones"), [Janine Turner](/wiki/Janine_Turner "Janine Turner"), [Red Buttons](/wiki/Red_Buttons "Red Buttons"), and [Eric Braeden](/wiki/Eric_Braeden "Eric Braeden") as the Doctor. [Kevin Hagen](/wiki/Kevin_Hagen "Kevin Hagen"), in his final film role released during his lifetime, plays a cop. In his first film role, [Stan Lee](/wiki/Stan_Lee "Stan Lee") of [Marvel Comics](/wiki/Marvel_Comics "Marvel Comics") has a small role as himself.

## Plot

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=The_Ambulance&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Plot")]

Aspiring comic book artist Josh Baker meets a young woman named Cheryl on the streets of [New York City](/wiki/New_York_City "New York City"), who proceeds to collapse and is rushed to a hospital by an ambulance. When Josh arrives at the hospital, he is shocked to find that there is no record of Cheryl ever being admitted. He soon learns another startling discovery: Cheryl's roommate also vanished after being picked up by the same ambulance.

Convinced that there is some sort of conspiracy going on, Josh proceeds to investigate the disappearances, despite the overt disdain and discouragement from Lt. Spencer.[[1]](#cite_note-1)

## Cast

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=The_Ambulance&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Cast")]

* [Eric Roberts](/wiki/Eric_Roberts "Eric Roberts") as Josh Baker
* [James Earl Jones](/wiki/James_Earl_Jones "James Earl Jones") as Lieutenant Frank Spencer
* [Megan Gallagher](/wiki/Megan_Gallagher "Megan Gallagher") as Officer Sandra Malloy
* [Red Buttons](/wiki/Red_Buttons "Red Buttons") as Elias Zacharai
* [Janine Turner](/wiki/Janine_Turner "Janine Turner") as Cheryl Turner
* [Eric Braeden](/wiki/Eric_Braeden "Eric Braeden") as The Doctor
* [Richard Bright](/wiki/Richard_Bright_(actor) "Richard Bright (actor)") as Detective Jerry McClosky
* James Dixon as Detective John "Jughead" Ryan
* Jill Gatsby as Jerilyn O'Brien
* Martin Barter as Street Gang Leader
* [Laurene Landon](/wiki/Laurene_Landon "Laurene Landon") as Patty
* [Nick Chinlund](/wiki/Nick_Chinlund "Nick Chinlund") as Hugo (credited as Nicholas Chinlund)
* [Beatrice Winde](/wiki/Beatrice_Winde "Beatrice Winde") as Head Nurse
* [Kevin Hagen](/wiki/Kevin_Hagen "Kevin Hagen") as Cop At Stables
* Matt Norklun as Ambulance Driver
* Rudy Jones as Ambulance Driver
* [Stan Lee](/wiki/Stan_Lee "Stan Lee") as Marvel Comics Editor
* Deborah Hedwall as Nurse Barbera Feinstein
* [Susan Blommaert](/wiki/Susan_Blommaert "Susan Blommaert") as Hospital Receptionist
* Jordan Derwin as Hospital Official
* Alexandra Jones as The Waitress
* [Michael O'Hare](/wiki/Michael_O%27Hare "Michael O'Hare") as Hal

## Production

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=The_Ambulance&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Production")]

Cohen later said he was inspired by "the concept of taking something that is thought of as being benign or benevolent... or anything else that has a safe and wholesome image, and turning it into an object of terror."[[2]](#cite_note-2) He had done this for *It's Alive* (babies) and *The Stuff* (junk food) and wanted to do it with ambulances. "When you hear or see an ambulance on the street, it's usually considered to be something that is going to rescue you and take care of you, a vehicle of mercy," said Cohen. "In this story, it's actually a vehicle of murder. The whole idea of an ambulance that suddenly arrives from nowhere, picks people up, and takes them away to some dark place where they are never seen or heard of again was completely original and creepy."[[3]](#cite_note-3)

Cohen says at one stage the financiers wanted a "classier" title than *The Ambulance* so he retitled it *In Thin Air*. They changed their mind and the film went back to being called *The Ambulance*.[[4]](#cite_note-4) In the May, 1990 edition of his column *Stan's Soapbox*, Stan Lee mentions playing himself in a scene in "an adventure mystery called *Into Thin Air"*[[5]](#cite_note-5) and refers to the name change in a later column.[[6]](#cite_note-6)

Cohen wanted to cast [John Travolta](/wiki/John_Travolta "John Travolta") or [Jim Carrey](/wiki/Jim_Carrey "Jim Carrey") in the lead but his producers refused.[[7]](#cite_note-7)

[Donald Trump](/wiki/Donald_Trump "Donald Trump") made a small cameo in a [deleted scene](/wiki/Deleted_scene "Deleted scene").

[Jamie Lee Curtis](/wiki/Jamie_Lee_Curtis "Jamie Lee Curtis") was originally considered to play Officer Sandra Malloy.

The role of the villain was originally played by [Wesley Addy](/wiki/Wesley_Addy "Wesley Addy"). However Cohen was unhappy with his performance and recast the role with Eric Braeden. Braeden's casting came at the suggestion of Cohen's mother who was a fan of *[The Young and the Restless](/wiki/The_Young_and_the_Restless "The Young and the Restless")*.[[8]](#cite_note-8)

## Home media

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=The_Ambulance&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Home media")]

The film was released on Blu-ray on March 13, 2018, by [Scream Factory](/wiki/Scream_Factory "Scream Factory").[[9]](#cite_note-9)
